revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Hathor-Fest]]
'''Hathor Festival''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== [[File:Egypt.Hathor.jpg|thumb|The goddess {{ill|Hathor|en|Hathor|qid=Q133343|lt=Hathor|1=Hathor}}]]
The '''Festival of Hathor''' (also known as '''"{{ill|Minor Festival of Bastet's Drunkenness|de|Kleines Bastet-Fest der Trunkenheit|ja|小さなバステトの酩酊祭|zh|小巴斯特醉酒节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minor Festival of Bastet's Drunkenness|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}"''') was celebrated annually in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} on the {{ill|19th and 20th of Achet I|de|19. und 20. Achet I|ja|アケト1月19日と20日|zh|阿赫特一月19日和20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19th and 20th of Achet I|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its origins date back to the {{ill|Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|de|Frühdynastik (Ägypten)|ja|初期王朝時代 (エジプト)|zh|早王朝时期（埃及）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Dynastic Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Evidence for the festival exists from the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}.
The 19th Achet I corresponds roughly to June 22, the {{ill|Solstice|en|Solstice|qid=Q123524|lt=summer solstice|1=Solstice}}, and coincided with the arrival of the {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|qid=Q1519552|lt=Nile flood|1=Flooding of the Nile}}. One day earlier, on the 18th Achet I, the {{ill|Festival of Osiris's Death|de|Totenfest des Osiris|1=Festival of Osiris|1=Wag Festival|2=de|3=Totenfest des Osiris|1=Festival of Osiris|4=fr|5=Fête Ouag|6=ja|7=オシリスの死者祭|8=zh|9=奥西里斯死亡节|lt=Wag Festival (Festival of the Death of Osiris)|ja|オシリスの死者祭|zh|奥西里斯死亡节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of Osiris's Death|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of Osiris|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was held, symbolizing the lowest point of the Nile and thus the death of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}.
Initially, the Festival of Hathor was associated only with the Nile flood, as the actual New Year's festival was celebrated on {{ill|Wepet-renpet|lt=1 Wepet-renpet|de|1. Wepet-renpet|ja|ウェペト・レンペト1日|zh|韦佩特-伦佩特1日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wepet-renpet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Due to {{ill|Precession|en|Precession|qid=Q104225|lt=precession|1=Precession}}, the date of the {{ill|heliacal rising|de|heliakisch|ja|ヘリアカルライジング|zh|偕日升|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heliacal rising|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Sirius|en|Sirius|qid=Q3409|lt=Sirius|1=Sirius}} shifted until around 2400 BCE to coincide with the Nile flood, resulting in the merging of the Bastet and Hathor festivals in {{ill|mythology|de|Mythologie|ja|神話学|zh|神话学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mythology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Mythology|qid=Q9134}}.
== Background == === Egyptian Mythology === [[File:Hathor cow.svg|thumb|Hathor as a celestial cow]]
In the "{{ill|Book of the Heavenly Cow|de|Buch von der Himmelskuh|ja|天の牝牛の書|zh|天牛之书|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Heavenly Cow|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}", Hathor plays a significant role as the bearer of the {{ill|Eye of Ra|en|Eye of Ra|qid=Q760218|lt=Eye of Ra|1=Eye of Ra}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|qid=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology|1=Egyptian mythology}}. Her father {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|qid=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}} commanded her, as the Eye of Ra, to annihilate rebellious humanity. Later regretting his decision, Ra tricked Hathor by flooding the land with beer dyed with {{ill|Ochre|en|Ochre|qid=Q194191|lt=ochre|1=ochre}}, resembling blood. Hathor dRank the beer, became intoxicated, and could not fulfill her destructive intent. The "Myth of the Heavenly Cow" describes this event: {{Quote|Then the fields were flooded three palms high by the majesty of this god (Ra). The goddess went out in the morning and found all flooded. Her face brightened, and she dRank, which pleased her heart. She returned drunk and could not recognize people anymore.|The Heavenly Cow<ref>{{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|zh|亚历山德拉·冯·利文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: "Wein, Weib und Gesang – Rituale für die Gefährliche Göttin." In: Carola Metzner-Nebelsick (Ed.): ''Rituale in der Vorgeschichte, Antike und Gegenwart.'' Rahden 2003, pp. 47–55, here p. 48.</ref>}}
Ra further commanded that alcoholic beverages be prepared in future celebrations of Hathor. Thus, this {{ill|Pourquoi story|en|Pourquoi story|qid=Q990560|lt=Just So Story|1=Pourquoi story}} became the foundation for the festivities.
=== Historical Context === From the mid-third millennium BCE, {{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|qid=Q930167|lt=Sopdet|1=Sopdet}} appeared as Sirius, rising simultaneously with the Nile flood. The dried earth then turned into ochre-colored liquid. The ochre explicitly originated from {{ill|Elephantine|en|Elephantine|qid=Q284009|lt=Elephantine|1=Elephantine}}, where {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|ja|エジプト学者|zh|埃及学家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト学|1=Egyptology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q145903}} locate the myth's origin.
== Festival Proceedings == === Hathor’s Welcoming Drink === {{ill|Liturgy|en|Liturgy|qid=Q172331|lt=Liturgical texts|1=Liturgy}} list the beverages served during celebrations. Upon Hathor's arrival, the ceremonial staff served a welcome drink in {{ill|Menu jars|de|Menu-Krug|ja|メヌの壺|zh|梅努壶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menu jars|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, accompanied by Egyptian hymns. Descriptions note a mixture of beer with special malt, water, {{ill|Phoenix (plant)|en|Phoenix (plant)|qid=Q27685|lt=date palm|1=Phoenix (plant)}} paste, {{ill|Myrrh|en|Myrrh|qid=Q203635|lt=myrrh|1=Myrrh}}, and fragrances.
Ochre was ritually added to reenact the celestial cow myth during the festival. Priests ceremonially transported Hathor's welcoming drink to a special temple chamber. Initially, this ritual applied only to the New Year's Festival but later expanded to other festivals.
Additionally, a type of red wine, a mix of {{ill|Liqueur|en|Liqueur|qid=Q178780|lt=liqueur|1=Liqueur}} and {{ill|Liquor|en|Liquor|qid=Q56139|lt=spirits|1=Liquor}}, was essential, known as "{{ill|Seth-wine|de|Seth-Wein|ja|セトのワイン|zh|赛特之酒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seth-wine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}", symbolizing enemies' blood. After the welcoming drink, priests offered Seth-wine to appease Hathor.
=== Observing Festival Rites === On the verso of the Cairo festival calendar, rites for the Hathor festival required participants to "make a great offering in the evening, wash with Nile water, apply cosmetics, anoint themselves with oils, and then get drunk on wine." Failure to observe rites incurred penalties.
=== Music and Singing === [[File:Hathor-Fest.jpg|thumb|Musicians at Hathor Festival]] Cult hymns had structured stanzas and refrains celebrating Hathor's return and seeking her protection. Instruments were divided into rhythm instruments ({{ill|Idiophone|en|Idiophone|qid=Q12639|lt=idiophones|1=Idiophone}} like {{ill|Sistrum|en|Sistrum|qid=Q426948|lt=sistrum|1=Sistrum}} and {{ill|Menat|en|Menat|qid=Q1779386|lt=menit|1=Menat}}, and {{ill|Membranophone|en|Membranophone|qid=Q212892|lt=membranophones|1=Membranophone}}) and string instruments ({{ill|Harp|en|Harp|qid=Q47369|lt=harp|1=Harp}}, {{ill|Lute|en|Lute|qid=Q180733|lt=lute|1=Lute}}, {{ill|Kithara|en|Kithara|qid=Q12634515|lt=lyre|1=Kithara}}).
Sistrum performances were key, with drums and strings reserved for special festivals. A chapel at the {{ill|Dendera Temple complex|en|Dendera Temple complex|qid=Q735254|lt=Temple of Hathor|1=Dendera Temple complex}} shows ten women musicians, including seven tambourine-playing Hathors. ==References==
{{translated page|de|Hathor-Fest|version=217003239|comment=Imported full de history}}
[[ja:ハトホル祭]] [[de:Hathor-Fest]]

{{wikidata link|Q1588895}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Hathor]] [[Category:History of beer]] [[Category:Mythology of food and drink]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
